operating_state: Total production reached record 1,805 MBOED in Q2 2023, increasing 7% year-over-year driven by Lower 48 development programs across Delaware Basin, Eagle Ford, Midland Basin and Bakken. Average realized price declined to $54.50 per BOE from $88.57 per BOE in Q2 2022, with crude oil prices down 32% and natural gas prices down 71% due to macroeconomic headwinds and higher global supplies.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased driven by higher production volumes and increased well work activity. DD&A expenses rose from higher production volumes and increased rates from reserve revisions reflecting higher operating expenses. Selling, general and administrative expenses increased primarily from mark-to-market adjustments on compensation programs, partially offset by reduced taxes from lower commodity prices.
material_events: Completed strategic debt refinancing extending weighted average maturity from 15 to 17 years. Closed North Field South joint venture in Qatar adding approximately 2 MTPA net LNG capacity and signed 20-year offtake agreements for approximately 2.2 MTPA at Saguero LNG facility in Mexico. Executed agreement to purchase remaining 50% interest in Surmont for approximately $3.0 billion subject to regulatory approvals. Returned $2.7 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
macro_exposures: Brent crude oil prices averaged $78.39 per barrel, down 31% from prior year due to macroeconomic headwinds and refinery outages concurrent with higher global supplies. Henry Hub natural gas prices averaged $2.09 per MMBTU, down 71% from higher North American production and excess inventories following mild winter. Company remains unhedged and continues monitoring Ukraine conflict impacts, OPEC Plus supplies, global demand, inventory levels, governmental policies, inflation and supply chain disruptions.
forward_outlook: Third-quarter 2023 production expected to be 1.78 to 1.82 MMBOED with full-year guidance raised to 1.80 to 1.81 MMBOED. Capital guidance narrowed with DD&A guidance increased. Reconfirmed 2023 planned return of capital to shareholders of $11 billion through three-tier framework, significantly exceeding 30% of anticipated operating cash flows goal.
uncertainty_profile: Surmont acquisition closing expected in second half 2023 subject to regulatory approvals while APLNG transaction subject to EIG's completion of Origin Energy acquisition and Australian regulatory approvals. Environmental accrual totaled $185 million for remediation activities with substantial expenditures expected within 30 years. Climate change regulations continue evolving with potential material impact on operations though specific compliance costs remain uncertain.
